Diodorus Cronus’s “master argument” was inspired by the discussion of future contingents in On Interpretation, a tract by this philosopher who introduced the syllogism and tutored Alexander the Great.
parts_sanitized: Arthur Prior dubbed Diodorus an "ancient Greek" version of this 20th-century philosopher, who wrote "Two Dogmas of Empiricism" and names an "indispensability argument" about math with Hilary Putnam.
Philo the Logician advocated a "material" analysis of these statements, according to which they are true only if their consequent is true whenever their antecedent is true.

Diodorus Cronus's "master argument" was inspired by the discussion of future contingents in On Interpretation, a tract by this philosopher who introduced the syllogism and tutored Alexander the Great.
answers: W.V. Quine [or W.V.O. Quine; or Willard Quine; or Willard Van Orman Quine]
answers_sanitized: W.V. Quine [or W.V.O. Quine; or Willard Quine; or Willard Van Orman Quine]
conditionals [or if-then statements; or material conditionals; or indicative conditionals]

69ebd8194997b65751097751.question: In 2013, Peter Sellars produced an unfinished semi-opera by this composer for which the artist Gronk creates Mayan-inspired art to blend with the Mesoamerican characters Montezuma and King Inca. This Marxist theory modifies Hegel’s dialectics by arguing that changes in real-world processes, not just ideas, lead to historical change. Stalin wrote a chapter on this theory and its historical counterpart.

69ebd8194997b65751097779.question: A book by this author suggests that the “mnemotechnics” (“NEM-oh-tek-niks”) of prehistoric man relied on the insight that “only that which never ceases to hurt stays in the memory.” This philosopher wrote that “the doer is merely a fiction added to the deed” in a book that analyzes the etymology of the word schuld(“SHOO-weld”) to argue that the feeling of guilt evolved from creditor-debtor relationships. This man argued that Jewish priests, driven by ressentiment (“RUH-sah-tee-MAH”), effectuated a metaphorical “slave revolt.” For 10 points, name this author who posited a “slave morality” in On the Genealogy of Morals and wrote in The Gay Science that “God is dead.”

For 10 points, name this author who posited a "slave morality" in On the Genealogy of Morals and wrote in The Gay Science that "God is dead."
